name: Bolognese Sauce
description: A rich and hearty Italian meat sauce slow-cooked with ground beef, pork, vegetables, and tomatoes. Perfect over fresh pasta.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add pancetta and cook until the fat renders, about 3-4 minutes.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 8 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for 1 more minute until fragrant.
Increase heat to medium-high. Add ground beef and ground pork, breaking up the meat as it cooks. Cook until browned, about 8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add tomato paste and stir to coat the meat. Cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in red wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Let the wine reduce by half, about 5 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, beef broth, oregano, basil, bay leaves, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Partially cover and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is thick and rich.
Stir in whole milk during the last 15 minutes of cooking.
Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Cook pasta according to package directions. Toss with sauce and serve topped with grated Parmesan.
notes: The sauce freezes well for up to 3 months. For an even richer flavor, simmer for up to 3 hours on very low heat.

name: Baked Ziti
description: A classic Italian-American comfort dish with ziti pasta layered with meat sauce, ricotta, and mozzarella, baked until bubbly and golden.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Cook ziti according to package directions until just al dente (1 minute less than package says). Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add ground beef and Italian sausage, breaking up the meat. Cook until browned, about 8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Stir in marinara sauce, crushed tomatoes, basil, oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes.
In a medium bowl, combine ricotta, egg, half the Parmesan, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Add the cooked pasta to the meat sauce and stir to combine.
Spread half the pasta mixture in the prepared baking dish.
Dollop the ricotta mixture evenly over the pasta layer. Sprinkle with 1 cup of mozzarella.
Top with the remaining pasta mixture. Sprinkle the remaining mozzarella and Parmesan over the top.
Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake an additional 20-25 minutes until bubbly and golden.
Let rest for 10 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh parsley.
notes: Can be assembled a day ahead and refrigerated before baking. Add 10 extra minutes to baking time if baking from cold.

name: Beef Broccoli Crockpot
description: Tender slices of beef slow-cooked in a savory ginger-soy sauce with crisp broccoli florets. A takeout favorite made easy at home.
instructions: Place sliced beef in the slow cooker.
In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, beef broth, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes.
Pour the sauce over the beef and stir to coat.
Cover and cook on LOW for 4-5 hours or HIGH for 2-3 hours, until beef is tender.
About 30 minutes before serving, whisk together cornstarch and water in a small bowl to make a slurry.
Stir the cornstarch slurry into the slow cooker and mix well.
Add broccoli florets on top of the beef. Cover and cook on HIGH for 20-30 minutes until broccoli is tender-crisp and the sauce has thickened.
Serve over steamed white rice, garnished with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
notes: For crispier broccoli, steam it separately and add just before serving. Leftovers keep well in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.

name: Beef Carnitas
description: Slow-braised beef that is shredded and crisped in the oven, seasoned with citrus, chilies, and warm spices. A bold twist on traditional pork carnitas.
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F.
In a small bowl, mix together cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper. Rub the spice mixture all over the beef chunks.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear beef chunks on all sides until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Work in batches to avoid crowding. Remove and set aside.
Add onion to the pot and cook for 2 minutes. Add garlic and chipotle peppers, cook for 1 minute more.
Pour in chicken broth, orange juice, and lime juice. Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the beef to the pot. Add bay leaves. The liquid should come about halfway up the meat.
Cover and braise in the oven for 3.5 to 4 hours, until the beef is fork-tender and easily shredded.
Remove beef from the pot and shred using two forks. Remove and discard bay leaves.
Preheat the broiler. Spread shredded beef on a large rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with several spoonfuls of the braising liquid.
Broil for 5-8 minutes until the edges of the beef are crispy. Watch closely to avoid burning.
Serve in warm tortillas topped with diced white onion and fresh cilantro.
notes: Leftover beef keeps well refrigerated for 4 days or frozen for 3 months. Delicious in burritos, burrito bowls, nachos, or over rice.

name: Beef Stroganoff
description: Tender strips of beef in a creamy mushroom and sour cream sauce served over egg noodles. A timeless Russian-inspired comfort dish.
instructions: Season beef strips with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Heat olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over high heat. Sear beef strips in batches, cooking 1-2 minutes per side until browned. Do not overcrowd. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Melt remaining 2 tablespoons butter in the skillet. Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms and cook until browned and most of the moisture has evaporated, about 6 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute. Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir to coat. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in beef broth while stirring constantly. Add Worcestershire sauce and Dijon mustard. Bring to a simmer and cook until slightly thickened, about 3-4 minutes.
Reduce heat to low. Stir in sour cream until smooth. Do not boil after adding sour cream or it may curdle.
Return beef and any accumulated juices to the pan. Stir gently and heat through, about 2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Meanwhile, cook egg noodles according to package directions. Drain well.
Serve stroganoff over egg noodles, garnished with fresh parsley.
notes: Use full-fat sour cream for the best texture. For a lighter version, substitute Greek yogurt. Leftovers reheat well over low heat with a splash of broth.

name: Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: Tender cabbage leaves filled with a seasoned beef and rice mixture, slow-simmered in a tangy tomato sauce. A beloved Eastern European comfort dish.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Core the cabbage and carefully submerge the whole head in the boiling water for 3-4 minutes until the outer leaves are pliable. Peel off 12-15 large leaves and set aside. Reserve the remaining cabbage.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, uncooked rice, half the diced onion, garlic, egg, 1 teaspoon salt, pepper, thyme, parsley, and paprika. Mix until just combined.

In a saucepan, combine crushed tomatoes, tomato sauce, tomato paste, beef broth, the remaining onion, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, and remaining 0.5 teaspoon salt. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Chop the remaining cabbage and spread it on the bottom of a large baking dish or Dutch oven. Pour about 1 cup of tomato sauce over the cabbage.

Place a cabbage leaf on a flat surface. Trim any thick vein if needed. Place about 1/3 cup of the meat mixture in the center. Fold in the sides and roll up tightly like a burrito. Place seam-side down in the baking dish.
Repeat with remaining leaves and meat mixture, packing rolls snugly in the dish.
Pour the remaining tomato sauce evenly over all the rolls, covering them completely.
Cover tightly with foil and bake for 1.5 hours until the meat is cooked through and the rice is tender.
Let rest 10 minutes before serving. Spoon sauce over the rolls when plating.
notes: These reheat beautifully the next day when the flavors have melded. Freeze well for up to 3 months. Use a mix of beef and pork for extra flavor.

name: Carne Asada
description: Citrus and garlic marinated flank steak grilled to perfection, then thinly sliced and served street-taco style with fresh toppings.
instructions: In a bowl or zip-top bag, whisk together orange juice, lime juice, olive oil, soy sauce, white vinegar, garlic, jalapeño, cilantro, cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Add the steak to the marinade, turning to coat on all sides. Seal and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight (up to 24 hours) for best flavor.
Remove steak from the refrigerator 30 minutes before grilling to bring to room temperature.
Preheat grill or grill pan to high heat. Oil the grates.
Remove steak from marinade and pat lightly with paper towels. Discard marinade.
Grill steak for 4-6 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temperature 130-135°F), or to desired doneness.
Transfer to a cutting board and let rest for 5-10 minutes.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain. Chop into smaller pieces if desired for tacos.
Warm corn tortillas on the grill or in a dry skillet.
Serve in warm tortillas topped with diced white onion, fresh cilantro, sliced avocado, salsa, and a squeeze of fresh lime.
notes: The longer the steak marinates, the more flavorful it will be. Always slice against the grain for the most tender result. Leftovers are great in burritos or over rice bowls.

name: Flank Steak with Chimichurri
description: A perfectly grilled flank steak served with a vibrant Argentinian chimichurri sauce made with fresh parsley, garlic, and olive oil.
instructions: Make the chimichurri: In a bowl, combine finely chopped parsley, cilantro, oregano, garlic, shallot, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, and red pepper flakes. Stir in 1/2 cup olive oil. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Let rest at least 15 minutes for flavors to meld.
Pat the flank steak dry with paper towels. Season generously on both sides with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Let the steak sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before cooking.
Preheat grill or cast-iron grill pan to high heat. Lightly oil the cooking surface.
Grill steak for 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temperature 130-135°F). Avoid moving the steak while it cooks to get good grill marks.
Transfer steak to a cutting board and tent loosely with foil. Let rest for at least 10 minutes.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain at a slight diagonal angle.
Arrange on a serving platter and spoon chimichurri sauce generously over the top. Serve with extra sauce on the side.
notes: Chimichurri can be made up to 3 days ahead and stored in the refrigerator. Bring to room temperature before serving. Great alongside roasted potatoes or grilled vegetables.

name: Instant Pot Beef Stew
description: A hearty, classic beef stew with tender chunks of beef, potatoes, carrots, and a rich, savory broth made in a fraction of the traditional time using the Instant Pot.
instructions: Season beef cubes with salt and pepper. Toss with flour to coat.
Set Instant Pot to SAUTE mode on high. Add olive oil. When hot, sear beef in batches until browned on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per batch. Remove and set aside.
Add onion and celery to the pot. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add garlic and tomato paste, cook for 1 minute.
Pour in beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. This is important to avoid a burn warning.
Return the beef to the pot. Add carrots, potatoes, thyme, rosemary, parsley, and bay leaves.
Secure the lid and set the valve to SEALING. Select MANUAL (or Pressure Cook) on HIGH pressure for 35 minutes.
When done, allow a natural pressure release for 15 minutes, then carefully turn the valve to VENTING to release any remaining steam.
Remove the lid. Discard bay leaves. Set the pot back to SAUTE mode.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and let the stew simmer for 3-5 minutes until thickened.
Stir in frozen peas and cook for 1 minute. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot.
notes: This stew tastes even better the next day. For a thicker stew, use more cornstarch slurry. Serve with crusty bread for dipping.

name: Korean Ground Beef Bowl
description: Savory and slightly sweet Korean-inspired ground beef served over steamed rice with a drizzle of sauce and fresh toppings. Ready in under 20 minutes.
instructions: Cook white rice according to package directions. Keep warm.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, gochujang, and rice wine vinegar. Set aside.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and cook, breaking it into small pieces, until browned and cooked through, about 7-8 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic and ginger to the beef. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour the sauce over the beef and stir to coat. Cook for 2 minutes until the sauce is absorbed and slightly caramelized. Season with black pepper.
If using, fry eggs in a separate pan to desired doneness.
Divide rice among bowls. Top with Korean ground beef, shredded carrots, cucumber slices, and a fried egg if desired.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately.
notes: This is a great meal prep recipe. Store the beef separately from the rice and toppings. Reheat beef in a pan with a splash of water. Leftovers keep for 4 days in the refrigerator.

name: Classic Meatloaf
description: A moist and flavorful old-fashioned meatloaf made with ground beef and pork, topped with a tangy sweet ketchup glaze. The ultimate comfort food.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a rimmed baking sheet or loaf pan with parchment paper or foil.
In a small bowl, combine breadcrumbs and milk. Let soak for 5 minutes to form a panade (this keeps the meatloaf moist).
Make the glaze: Whisk together 1/2 cup ketchup, brown sugar, and apple cider vinegar in a small bowl. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine ground beef, ground pork, onion, garlic, eggs, soaked breadcrumbs, 3 tablespoons ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, parsley, salt, pepper, paprika, and thyme.
Mix with your hands until just combined. Do not over-mix or the meatloaf will be tough.
Transfer the meat mixture to the prepared pan. Shape into a loaf about 9 inches long and 4 inches wide.
Spread half the glaze over the top and sides of the loaf.
Bake for 45 minutes. Spread the remaining glaze over the top and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, until the internal temperature reaches 160°F.
Let the meatloaf rest for 10 minutes before slicing. This helps it hold its shape.
Slice and serve with mashed potatoes and green beans.
notes: Using a mix of beef and pork adds moisture and flavor. For a free-form loaf baked on a sheet pan, the outside gets crispier. Leftovers make excellent meatloaf sandwiches the next day.

name: Mississippi Chuck Roast
description: An incredibly tender and flavorful slow-cooked chuck roast made with pepperoncini peppers, ranch seasoning, and butter. Only 5 ingredients and practically zero effort.
instructions: Pat the chuck roast dry with paper towels. Season on all sides with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Optional but recommended: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat with a splash of oil. Sear the roast for 3-4 minutes per side until well browned. This adds extra depth of flavor.
Place the roast in the slow cooker.
Sprinkle the ranch seasoning packet and au jus gravy packet evenly over the top of the roast.
Place the sliced butter on top of the seasoning.
Add the whole pepperoncini peppers around and on top of the roast. Pour in the pepperoncini brine.
Do NOT add any additional liquid. The roast and butter will create plenty of liquid as it cooks.
Cover and cook on LOW for 8-10 hours or HIGH for 4-5 hours, until the meat is fall-apart tender.
Shred the beef directly in the slow cooker using two forks, mixing it with all the juices.
Serve the shredded beef on toasted hoagie rolls topped with provolone cheese and some of the pepperoncini peppers. Dip the sandwich in the cooking juices.
notes: Do not skip the pepperoncini brine — it adds incredible tangy flavor without making the dish spicy. Leftovers are fantastic over mashed potatoes or egg noodles.
